site.title: Miriam González
site.subtitle: Searching for a Precision Oncology with AI

hero.tagline: One patient. A unique tumor. A real chance.
hero.title: Miriam has metastatic breast cancer and standard medicine has no answers for her case.
hero.subtitle: Her tumor doesn't respond to any standard treatment. Finding the right one requires tests that public healthcare won't fund. And time cannot wait.

gofundme.title: Why do we need your help?
gofundme.subtitle: Miriam needs to fund a precision diagnostic strategy that public healthcare doesn't cover. Every contribution brings her case closer to an answer — and could set a precedent for other patients with tumours outside clinical guidelines.
gofundme.what_intro: The money covers the needs of the case as they emerge — not just one test, but everything involved in finding the right treatment for a tumour with no established protocol.
what_items: Bone re-biopsy with advanced genomic panel
Functional immunohistochemistry tests to identify therapeutic targets
Consultations and second opinions with international specialists
Clinical documentation for access to trials and compassionate use programmes
Any additional test that arises throughout the process
gofundme.why_title: Why isn't this covered by public healthcare?
gofundme.why_text: Current clinical guidelines classify this tumour as "standard HR+", but its real biology — with 80% neuroendocrine differentiation and FGFR1 ×13 amplification — needs precision tests that are not included in the protocol. Without them, the treatment is insufficient for what Miriam actually has.
gofundme.transparency_text: Every expense is publicly documented. We request quotes from international reference centers and share them with the community.

science.title: The science behind the case
science.subtitle: An ultra-rare molecular profile that challenges current clinical guidelines

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.subtitle: Week by week, every milestone matters
timeline.notice: This timeline is updated weekly. You can follow the day-to-day in real time on X/Twitter.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: If you're an oncology professional, researcher, journalist or simply want to help
contact.message: If you have relevant information about clinical trials, medical contacts, or you'd like to collaborate in any way, don't hesitate to reach out.
contact.for_professionals_desc: If you're interested in the case from a clinical or research perspective, we can share the complete clinical documentation (clinical summary v4, proposed panel, references) confidentially.
contact.message_placeholder: How can you help or what information do you have?
contact.privacy_notice: Your data is only used to respond to your message. It is not shared with third parties.

footer.disclaimer: This site is an informational resource maintained by the patient's support team. It does not replace professional medical advice.
footer.made_with: Made with care, science, and a lot of AI.

ciencia.proposed_panel_caption: Proposed re-biopsy panel: components, methods, targets and clinical implication
ciencia.goal_n_of_1_desc: A trial where Miriam is the sole unit of analysis, with therapeutic decisions guided by her specific molecular profile rather than "generic HR+". The path: re-biopsy with advanced panel → MTB at WIN Consortium international board for a molecularly-directed N-of-1 trial with AI.
ciencia.win_consortium_desc: WIN Consortium (Worldwide Innovative Network in Oncology) is an international network connecting centres of excellence in precision oncology to design personalised diagnostic and therapeutic strategies.
ciencia.win_consortium_precedent: This would set a precedent: precision oncology adapted to each patient, without bureaucracy around the primary tumor.
ciencia.why_no_fit_guidelines: Why doesn't this tumor fit the guidelines?
ciencia.why_no_fit_guidelines_text: Formally it is a luminal breast cancer (HR+), but with ~80% neuroendocrine differentiation and an FGFR1 ×13. Guidelines tend to recommend treating it as standard luminal, minimizing the neuroendocrine component. Literature and clinical reasoning suggest this hybrid subtype (BC-NED) has worse prognosis and may require completely different strategies.
ciencia.thesis: The thesis: biology, not the organ of origin, should guide treatment.
ciencia.thesis_goal: Goal: an N-of-1 trial that sets a precedent for precision oncology in ultra-rare subtypes.

team.title: The team
team.subtitle: A multidisciplinary group that formed spontaneously around a common goal
team.how_it_formed: This team wasn't planned. It formed organically when professionals from different fields (technology, medicine, AI, research) saw a case that challenged them and decided to contribute what they knew. There is no formal hierarchy. There is a shared goal: let this tumor's biology guide treatment.

historia.fallback_notice: This chapter is not yet available in English. Showing the Spanish version.
historia.pending_notice: This section is being written by someone close to Miriam. It will tell her story from before the diagnosis: who she is beyond cancer, how she got here, and why what she's doing matters. Coming soon.
historia.story_teaser_1: Three years ago, Miriam was working full-time as a software developer and creating content about technology. Within a matter of months, her life took a radical turn.
historia.story_teaser_2: It all began with back pain, physiotherapy sessions and GPs who couldn't find a solution. As the pain persisted and the healthcare system offered no answers, she decided to arrange an MRI scan herself. One Thursday in January 2024, she found the test results in her inbox: «probable vertebral metastases». A couple of weeks later, the definitive diagnosis arrived: «Stage IV breast cancer with neuroendocrine differentiation».
historia.story_teaser_3: For two years, Miriam has been rebuilding her life piece by piece with treatments that have given her time and peace of mind, but with an end date. In 2026, everything has moved forward at breakneck speed. What if these advances, combined with the specific nature of her cancer, opened doors that previously seemed closed?
historia.story_teaser_4: In this story, we want to tell you about Míriam's case, her journey and her daily routine so that you can truly understand what it means to live with stage 4 cancer.
